name: Baja Pizza Pouch (Calzones)
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a large skillet over medium heat until hot.
2. Crumble the pork sausage and ground beef into the skillet.
3. Cook, stirring to separate the meat, until no pink remains. Drain off fat.
4. Add beans, cumin, and garlic powder; heat through.
5. Oil two baking sheets.
6. Preheat oven to 400°F.
7. Prepare hot roll mix according to package instructions.
8. Divide dough into 8 pieces. Form each piece into a ball.
9. On lightly floured surface, roll each ball into a 7-inch circle.
10. Place on prepared baking sheets.
11. Spread 1/2 cup meat mixture onto 1/2 of each dough circle, keeping it within 1/2 inch from the edge.
12. Layer each calzone with 2 tablespoons crushed tomatoes, 1/4 cup Monterey Jack cheese, and 2 tablespoons shredded Cheddar cheese.
13. Moisten the edges of the dough with water.
14. Fold over to enclose the filling, pressing edges firmly together with a fork.
15. Brush with melted butter.
16. Cut slits in each calzone to allow steam to escape.
17. Bake for 18 to 20 minutes or until golden brown.
18. Serve with guacamole, if desired.
